description: Move your shoulders back behind your ears. Arch your upper and middle back, aiming to touch the wall with your chest.
Slowly move your neck to its maximum range in all four directions, then gently rotate left and right to release tension.
Simultaneously rotate your wrist and the opposite ankle to enhance joint flexibility.
Rotate your torso to each side, allowing your head and elbow to lead the movement. Engage your core to control and limit the rotation at its maximum range.

Sit down with your legs apart, in a comfortable position. Keep your chest open at all times. Try any of these stretches and rotations—they help relieve stiffness in the lower back while also stretching your hamstrings.
